content: flutter_rust_bridge 2.x (FRB2) generates async Dart bindings automatically: any Rust async fn becomes a Future<T> in Dart; synchronous Rust fn also works as Dart Future (runs on a separate thread pool managed by FRB). For streaming: a Rust function that returns RustStream<T> (backed by a tokio channel or std mpsc) is exposed as a Dart Stream<T>. FRB2 uses a Dart isolate worker pool so long-running Rust calls never block the main UI isolate. The bridged functions are called via auto-generated Dart API: api.myRustFn(args) returns Future. For progress: the recommended FRB2 pattern is to use DartFnFuture (callback from Rust into Dart) or to expose a Rust function that takes a StreamSink<ProgressEvent> parameter — FRB2 serializes calls across the isolate boundary automatically. The bridge layer generates: dart/frb_generated.dart (Dart glue code), src/frb_generated.rs (Rust glue code). The Rust crate must add crate-type = [cdylib, staticlib] in Cargo.toml.
